_annotation: This is an annotated example. Copy .github/mcp-config.json to your repo and replace values. Remove _annotation fields before committing.
description: Approved MCP server registry. Default policy: deny. Only servers listed here may be called.

policy._annotation_policy: Always set default_action to deny. Require CODEOWNERS approval to add servers.

servers._annotation: Server 1: GitHub REST API — read-only. Safe for most agent workflows.
servers.name: github-rest
servers.description: GitHub REST API — read-only access to issues, PRs, and code.
servers._annotation_auth: GITHUB_TOKEN is short-lived and scoped per workflow run. Prefer it over PATs.
servers._annotation_scopes: List denied_scopes explicitly. It makes the threat model reviewable.

network_policy._annotation_network: Restrict to the minimum hostnames. allow_internet: false is the safe default.

servers.threat_model_note: Short-lived GITHUB_TOKEN; read-only scopes. Risk: token in logs — mitigated by masking.

servers._annotation: Server 2: GitHub Actions API — allows triggering workflow_dispatch events.
servers.name: github-actions
servers.description: GitHub Actions API — trigger workflow dispatches and read run logs.
servers._annotation_actions_write: actions:write allows triggering workflows. Gate on non-production environments only.
servers.threat_model_note: actions:write can trigger arbitrary workflows — limit to workflow_dispatch on non-production environments.

servers._annotation: Server 3: Custom internal tool stub. Replace all placeholder values.
servers.name: YOUR_TOOL_NAME
servers.description: REPLACE: one-sentence description of what this server provides.
servers.auth_method: REPLACE_WITH: GITHUB_TOKEN | PAT | OIDC | API_KEY | NONE
servers.threat_model_note: REPLACE: describe the highest-risk action and its mitigation.

deny_list.note: Default deny — all servers not in the allow list are blocked.
Block cloud IMDS endpoint — prevents credential exfiltration.
